title: Gene therapy dna vector
abstract: The invention refers to genetic engineering and can be used in biotechnology, medicine, and agriculture for the manufacture of gene therapy products. The specified purpose is achieved by using gene therapy DNA vectors are created based on gene therapy DNA vector VTvaflV for the treatment of diseases featuring disruption of blastocyst adhesion, attachment, implantation, and apposition mechanism, female infertility development caused by endometrium failure during the blastocyst implantation period, including during the in vitro fertilization procedures, via the increase of the expression of IL11, LIT, DICER, HOXA10, or WT1 therapeutic genes in humans and animals.
claims:
Claims 1. Gene therapy DNA vectors based on gene therapy DNA vector VTvafl7 for the treatment of diseases featuring disruption of blastocyst adhesion, attachment, implantation, and apposition mechanism, female infertility development caused by endometrium failure during the blastocyst implantation period, including during the in vitro fertilization procedures, via the increase of the expression of IL11 therapeutic gene in humans and animals, while the gene therapy DNA vector has the coding region of IL11 therapeutic gene cloned to gene therapy DNA vector VTvafl7 resulting in gene therapy DNA vector VTvafl7-ILl 1 that has nucleotide sequence SEQ ID No. 1. 2. Gene therapy DNA vectors based on gene therapy DNA vector VTvafl7 for the treatment of diseases featuring disruption of blastocyst adhesion, attachment, implantation, and apposition mechanism, female infertility development caused by endometrium failure during the blastocyst implantation period, including during the in vitro fertilization procedures, via the increase of the expression of LIF therapeutic gene in humans and animals, while the gene therapy DNA vector has the coding region of LIF therapeutic gene cloned to gene therapy DNA vector VTvafl7 resulting in gene therapy DNA vector VTvafl7-LIF that has nucleotide sequence SEQ ID No. 2. 3. Gene therapy DNA vectors based on gene therapy DNA vector VTvafl7 for the treatment of diseases featuring disruption of blastocyst adhesion, attachment, implantation, and apposition mechanism, female infertility development caused by endometrium failure during the blastocyst implantation period, including during the in vitro fertilization procedures, via the increase of the expression of DICER therapeutic gene in humans and animals, while the gene therapy DNA vector has the coding region of DICER therapeutic gene cloned to gene therapy DNA vector VTvafl7 resulting in gene therapy DNA vector VTvafl7-DICER that has nucleotide sequence SEQ ID No. 3. 4. Gene therapy DNA vectors based on gene therapy DNA vector VTvafl7 for the treatment of diseases featuring disruption of blastocyst adhesion, attachment, implantation, and apposition mechanism, female infertility development caused by endometrium failure during the blastocyst implantation period, including during the in vitro fertilization procedures, via the increase of the expression of HOXA10 therapeutic gene in humans and animals, while the gene therapy DNA vector has the coding region of HOXA10 therapeutic gene cloned to gene therapy DNA vector VTvafl7 resulting in 

 gene therapy DNA vector VTvafl 7-HOXA 10 that has nucleotide sequence SEQ ID No. 4. 5. Gene therapy DNA vectors based on gene therapy DNA vector VTvafl7 for the treatment of diseases featuring disruption of blastocyst adhesion, attachment, implantation, and apposition mechanism, female infertility development caused by endometrium failure during the blastocyst implantation period, including during the in vitro fertilization procedures, via the increase of the expression of WT1 therapeutic gene in humans and animals, while the gene therapy DNA vector has the coding region of WT1 therapeutic gene cloned to gene therapy DNA vector VTvafl7 resulting in gene therapy DNA vector VTvafl7-WTl that has nucleotide sequence SEQ ID No. 5. 6. Gene therapy DNA vector based on gene therapy DNA vector VTvafl7 carrying the IL11, or LIF, or DICER, or HOXAIO, or WT1 therapeutic gene as per claim 1, 2, 3, 4, or 5. Said DNA vector is unique due to the fact that each of the constructed gene therapy DNA vectors: VTvafl7-ILl l, or VTvafl7-LIF, or VTvafl 7- DICER, or VTvafl7-HOXA10, or VTvafl7-WTl as per claim 1, 2, 3, 4, or 5 due to the limited size of VTvafl7 vector part not exceeding 3200 bp, has the ability to efficiently penetrate into human and animal cells and express the IL11, or LIF, or DICER, or HOXAIO, or WT1 genes therapeutic gene cloned to it. 7. Gene therapy DNA vector based on gene therapy DNA vector VTvafl7 carrying the IL11, or LIF, or DICER, or HOXAIO, or WT1 therapeutic gene as per claim 1, 2, 3, 4, or 5. Said DNA vector is unique due to the fact that each of the constructed gene therapy DNA vectors: VTvafl7-ILl l, or VTvafl7-LIF, or VTvafl7- DICER, or VTvafl7-HOXA10, or VTvafl7-WTl as per claim 1, 2, 3, 4, or 5 uses nucleotide sequences that are not antibiotic resistance genes, virus genes, or regulatory elements of viral genomes, which ensures its safe use for gene therapy in humans and animals. 8. A method of production of gene therapy DNA vector based on gene therapy DNA vector VTvafl7 carrying the IL11, or LIF, or DICER, or HOXAIO, or WT1 therapeutic gene as per claim 1, 2, 3, 4, or 5 that involves obtaining of each of the group of gene therapy DNA vectors: VTvafl7-ILl 1, or VTvafl7-LIF, or VTvafl 7-DICER, or VTvafl7-HOXA10, or VTvafl7-WTl as follows: the coding region of the IL11, or LIF, or DICER, or HOXAIO, or WT1 therapeutic gene is cloned to DNA vector VTvafl 7, 

 and gene therapy DNA vector VTvafl7-ILl 1, SEQ ID NÂ°l, or VTvafl7-LIF, SEQ ID NÂ°2, or VTvafl 7-DICER, SEQ ID NÂ°3, or VTvafl 7-HOXA 10, SEQ ID NÂ°4, or VTvafl7-WTl, SEQ ID NÂ°5, respectively, is obtained, while the coding region of the IL11, or LIF, or DICER, or HOXAIO, or WT1 therapeutic gene is obtained by isolating total RNA from a human biological tissue sample followed by the reverse transcription reaction and PCR amplification using specific oligonucleotides and cleaving the amplification product by restriction endonucleases, and cloning to the gene therapy DNA vector VTvafl 7 is carried out at Sail and Kpnl restriction sites or at BamElI and EcoRI restriction sites, while the selection is performed without antibiotics, at the same time, the following oligonucleotides produced for this purpose are used during gene therapy DNA vector VTvafl7-ILl 1, SEQ ID No. 1 production for the reverse transcription reaction and PCR amplification: IL1 I F AGG AT C CCTGT GGGGAC AT G AACT GT G, IL11_R AGT G A ATT CT C AC AGCCG AGT CTT C AGC, and the cleaving of amplification product and cloning of the coding region of IL11 gene to DNA vector VTvafl 7 is performed using BamHI and EcoRI restriction endonucleases, and the following oligonucleotides produced for this purpose are used during gene therapy DNA vector VTvafl 7-LIF, SEQ ID No. 2 production for the reverse transcription reaction and PCR amplification: LIF F AGG ATCC ACC AT G AAGGTCTT GGC GGC AG, LIF R TAT G A ATT CT AG AAGGCCT GGGCC A AC , and the cleaving of amplification product and cloning of the coding region of LIF gene to DNA vector VTvafl 7 is performed using BamHI and EcoRI restriction endonucleases, and the following oligonucleotides produced for this purpose are used during gene therapy DNA vector VTvafl 7-DICER, SEQ ID No. 3 production for the reverse transcription reaction and PCR amplification: DICER F ATCGTCGACGAGGAGATCTGCCGCCGCGATCGCC, 

 DICER R T CGGTACCTAGCT ATT GGG A ACCT GAGGTT G, and the cleaving of amplification product and cloning of the coding region of DICER gene to DNA vector VTvafl7 is performed using Sail and Kpnl restriction endonucleases, and the following oligonucleotides produced for this purpose are used during gene therapy DNA vector VTvafl7-HOXA10, SEQ ID No. 4 production for the reverse transcription reaction and PCR amplification: HOXA10 F CCGATATCGAGGAGATCTGCCGCCGCGATCGCC, HOXA1 O R GGG A ATT CT AGGA A AA ATT A A AGTT GGCTG, and the cleaving of amplification product and cloning of the coding region of HOXAIO gene to DNA vector VTvafl7 is performed using EcoRV and EcoRI restriction endonucleases, and the following oligonucleotides produced for this purpose are used during gene therapy DNA vector VTvafl7-WTl, SEQ ID No. 5 production for the reverse transcription reaction and PCR amplification: WT1JF ATCGTCGACTGGATCCGGTACCGAGGAGATCTG, WT 1 R GGG AATT CTA A AGCGCC AGCT GG AGTTT G, and the cleaving of amplification product and cloning of the coding region of WT1 gene to DNA vector VTvafl7 is performed using Sail and EcoRI restriction endonucleases. 9. The method of usage of gene therapy DNA vector based on gene therapy DNA vector VTvafl7 carrying the IL11, or LIF, or DICER, or HOXAIO, or WT1 therapeutic gene as per claim 1, 2, 3, 4, or 5 for the treatment of diseases featuring disruption of blastocyst adhesion, attachment, implantation, and apposition mechanism, female infertility development caused by endometrium failure during the blastocyst implantation period, including during the in vitro fertilization procedures, via the increase of the expression of the IL11, or LIF, or DICER, or HOXAIO, or WT1 therapeutic gene in humans and animals, that involves transfection of cells of human or animal organs and tissues with the selected gene therapy DNA vector carrying the therapeutic gene based on gene therapy DNA vector VTvafl7 or several selected gene 

 therapy DNA vectors carrying the therapeutic genes based on gene therapy DNA vector VTvafl7 of the constructed gene therapy DNA vectors carrying the IL11, or LIF, or DICER, or HOXA10, or WT1 therapeutic genes based on gene therapy DNA vector VTvafl7, and/or injection of human or animal autologous cells of said patient or animal transfected with the selected gene therapy DNA vector carrying the therapeutic gene based on gene therapy DNA vector VTvafl7 or several selected gene therapy DNA vectors carrying the therapeutic genes based on gene therapy DNA vector VTvafl7 of the constructed gene therapy DNA vectors carrying therapeutic genes based on gene therapy DNA vector VTvafl7 into human or animal organs and tissues, and/or injection of organs and tissues of the patient or animal with the selected gene therapy DNA vector carrying the therapeutic gene based on gene therapy DNA vector VTvafl7 or several selected gene therapy DNA vectors carrying the therapeutic genes based on gene therapy DNA vector VTvafl7 of the constructed gene therapy DNA vectors carrying therapeutic genes based on gene therapy DNA vector VTvafl7, or a combination of the indicated methods. 10. The method of production of strain for construction of a gene therapy DNA vector as per claim 1, 2, 3, 4 or 5 for the treatment of diseases featuring disruption of blastocyst adhesion, attachment, implantation, and apposition mechanism, female infertility development caused by endometrium failure during the blastocyst implantation period, including during the in vitro fertilization procedures, via the increase of the expression of the IL11, or LIF, or DICER, or HOXA10, or WT1 therapeutic gene in humans and animals that involves making electrocompetent cells of Escherichia coli strain SCS110-AF and subjecting these cells to electroporation with gene therapy DNA vector VTvafl7-ILl 1, or DNA vector VTvafl7-LIF, or DNA vector VTvafl 7-DICER, or DNA vector VTvafl7-HOXA10, or DNA vector VTvafl7-WTl. After that, the cells are poured into agar plates (Petri dishes) with a selective medium containing yeastrel, peptone, 6% sucrose, and 10pg/ml of chloramphenicol, and as a result, Escherichia coli strain SCS110-AF/VTvafl7-ILl l, or Escherichia coli strain SCS110-AF/VTvafl7-LIF, or Escherichia coli strain SCSl lO-AF/VTvafl 7-DICER, or Escherichia coli strain SCS110-AF/VTvafl7-HOXA10, or Escherichia coli strain SCS110-AF/VTvafl7-WTl is obtained. 11. Escherichia coli strain SCS110-AF/VTvafl7-ILl l obtained as per claim 10 carrying the gene therapy DNA vector VTvafl 7-IL11 for its production allowing for 

 antibiotic-free selection during the production of the gene therapy DNA vector for the treatment of diseases featuring disruption of blastocyst adhesion, attachment, implantation, and apposition mechanism, female infertility development caused by endometrium failure during the blastocyst implantation period, including during the in vitro fertilization procedures, via the increase of the expression of the IL11 therapeutic gene in humans and animals. 12. Escherichia coli strain SCSI 10-AF/VTvafl7-LIF obtained as per claim 10 carrying the gene therapy DNA vector VTvafl7-LIF for its production allowing for antibiotic-free selection during the production of the gene therapy DNA vector for the treatment of diseases featuring disruption of blastocyst adhesion, attachment, implantation, and apposition mechanism, female infertility development caused by endometrium failure during the blastocyst implantation period, including during the in vitro fertilization procedures, via the increase of the expression of the LIF therapeutic gene in humans and animals. 13. Escherichia coli strain SCSI 10-AF/VTvafl 7-DICER obtained as per claim 10 carrying the gene therapy DNA vector VTvafl 7-DICER for its production allowing for antibiotic-free selection during the production of the gene therapy DNA vector for the treatment of diseases featuring disruption of blastocyst adhesion, attachment, implantation, and apposition mechanism, female infertility development caused by endometrium failure during the blastocyst implantation period, including during the in vitro fertilization procedures, via the increase of the expression of the DICER therapeutic gene in humans and animals. 14. Escherichia coli strain SCSI 10-AF/VTvafl7-HOXA10 obtained as per claim 10 carrying the gene therapy DNA vector VTvafl 7-HOXA10 for its production allowing for antibiotic-free selection during the production of the gene therapy DNA vector for the treatment of diseases featuring disruption of blastocyst adhesion, attachment, implantation, and apposition mechanism, female infertility development caused by endometrium failure during the blastocyst implantation period, including during the in vitro fertilization procedures, via the increase of the expression of the HOXA10 therapeutic gene in humans and animals. 15. Escherichia coli strain SCS110-AF/VTvafl7-WTl obtained as per claim 10 carrying the gene therapy DNA vector VTvafl 7-WT1 for its production allowing for 

 antibiotic-free selection during the production of the gene therapy DNA vector for the treatment of diseases featuring disruption of blastocyst adhesion, attachment, implantation, and apposition mechanism, female infertility development caused by endometrium failure during the blastocyst implantation period, including during the in vitro fertilization procedures, via the increase of the expression of the WT1 therapeutic gene in humans and animals. 16. A method of production on an industrial scale of gene therapy DNA vector VTvafl7 carrying the IL11, or LIF, or DICER, or HOXAIO, or WT1 therapeutic gene as per claim 1, 2, 3, 4 or 5 for treatment of diseases featuring disruption of blastocyst adhesion, attachment, implantation, and apposition mechanism, female infertility development caused by endometrium failure during the blastocyst implantation period, including during the in vitro fertilization procedures, via the increase of the expression of the IL11, or LIF, or DICER, or HOXAIO, or WT1 therapeutic gene in humans and animals that involves production of gene therapy DNA vector VTvafl7-ILl 1, or gene therapy DNA vector VTvafl7-LIF, or gene therapy DNA vector VTvafl 7-DICER, or gene therapy DNA vector VTvafl 7-HOXAlO, or gene therapy DNA vector VTvafl 7- WT1 by inoculating a culture flask containing the prepared medium with seed culture selected from Escherichia coli strain SCSI 10-AF/VTvafl7-ILl 1, or Escherichia coli strain SCS 110-AF/VTvaf 17-LIF, or Escherichia coli strain SCS110-AF/VTvafl7- DICER, or Escherichia coli strain SCSI 10-AF/VTvafl7-HOXA10, or Escherichia coli strain SCSI 10-AF/VTvafl7-WTl , then the cell culture is incubated in an incubator shaker and transferred to an industrial fermenter, then grown to a stationary phase, then the fraction containing the target DNA product is extracted, multi-stage filtered, and purified by chromatographic methods.
